title: Why Play Matters More Than STEM
subtitle: 比STEM更重要的，是让孩子去玩

paragraphs.en: Educators and business leaders have more in common than it may seem. Teachers want to prepare students for a successful future. Technology companies have an interest in developing a workforce with the STEM (science, technology, engineering and math) skills needed to grow the company and advance the industry.
paragraphs.zh: 教育工作者与商业领袖之间的共同点比看起来要多。教师希望学生为成功的未来做好准备。科技公司则有意培养具备STEM（科学、技术、工程和数学）技能的劳动力，以推动公司成长和行业进步。

paragraphs.en: How can they work together to achieve these goals? Play may be the answer. Focusing on STEM skills is important, but the reality is that STEM skills are enhanced and more relevant when combined with traditional, hands-on creative activities.
paragraphs.zh: 他们如何携手实现这些目标？答案可能是玩耍。注重STEM技能固然重要，但现实情况是，当STEM技能与传统动手创意活动相结合时，会得到强化并更具实际意义。

words.meaning: adj. 动手操作的，实际参与的

paragraphs.en: This combination is proving to be the best way to prepare today's children to be the makers and builders of tomorrow. That is why technology companies are partnering with educators to bring back good, old-fashioned play.
paragraphs.zh: 这种结合正被证明是培养当今儿童成为未来创造者与建设者的最佳途径。正因如此，科技公司正在与教育工作者合作，重新倡导优质的传统玩耍方式。

paragraphs.en: In fact, many experts argue that the most important 21st-century skills aren't related to specific technologies or subject matter, but to creativity — skills like imagination, problem-finding and problem-solving, teamwork, optimism, patience and the ability to experiment and take risks. These are skills acquired when kids tinker.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，许多专家认为，21世纪最重要的技能并非与特定技术或学科内容相关，而是与创造力相关——比如想象力、发现和解决问题的能力、团队合作、乐观、耐心以及尝试和冒险的能力。这些正是孩子们在动手摆弄时获得的技能。

paragraphs.en: High-tech industries such as NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory have found that their best overall problem solvers were master tinkerers in their youth. There are cognitive benefits of doing things the way we did as children — building something, tearing it down, then building it up again.
paragraphs.zh: NASA喷气推进实验室等高科技机构发现，他们最出色的综合问题解决者在年少时都是动手达人。像孩童时代那样做事——搭建、拆除、再重建——能带来认知上的益处。

paragraphs.en: Research shows that given 15 minutes of free play, four- and five-year-olds will spend a third of this time engaged in spatial, mathematical, and architectural activities. This type of play — especially with building blocks — helps children discover and develop key principles in math and geometry.
paragraphs.zh: 研究表明，在15分钟的自由玩耍中，四五岁的孩子会将三分之一的时间用于空间、数学和建筑类活动。这类玩耍——尤其是搭积木——有助于孩子发现并掌握数学与几何的关键原理。

words.meaning: adj. 空间的，空间感知的

paragraphs.en: If play and building are critical to 21st-century skill development, that's really good news for two reasons: Children are born builders, makers, and creators, so fostering 21st-century skills may be as simple as giving kids room to play, tinker and try things out, even as they grow older. Secondly, it doesn't take 21st-century technology to foster 21st-century skills.
paragraphs.zh: 如果玩耍和搭建对21世纪技能发展至关重要，那这确实是个好消息，原因有二：孩子生来就是建造者、制造者和创造者，因此培养21世纪技能或许只需给孩子空间去玩耍、摆弄和尝试，即使他们年龄渐长也是如此。其次，培养21世纪技能并不需要21世纪的技术。

paragraphs.en: This is especially important for under-resourced schools and communities. Taking whatever materials are handy and tinkering with them is a simple way to engage those important "maker" skills. And anyone, anywhere, can do it.
paragraphs.zh: 这对资源匮乏的学校和社区尤为重要。随手拿起身边的任何材料进行摆弄，就是调动那些重要“创客”技能的简单方法。而且任何人、在任何地方都能做到。
